title: equisetum arvense subsp. alpestre
intro: Equisetum arvense subsp. alpestre, also known as alpine horse tail, is a subspecies of the Equisetum arvense plant, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the genus Equisetum and is known for its unique, hollow, and jointed stems. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate conditions such as edema and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Equisetum arvense subsp. alpestre include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
